perturbation.original_text: Other Confidential Information disclosed orally shall be thereafter summarized by the Disclosing party and the summary provided to the Receiving Party clearly identifying that such information is other party that was not known by such party or commonly known in the public domain shall be presumed to be other confidential for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Other Confidential Information disclosed orally shall not need to be summarized by the Disclosing party and provided to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the requirement for oral disclosures of confidential information to be summarized in writing. By removing the summarization step, it creates uncertainty about whether oral disclosures are properly documented and treated as confidential, directly contradicting the intent of Section 2: Identification to properly identify any confidential information and also creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Identification. The disclosing Party shall identify the written Other Confidential Information or any other Confidential Information disclosed in tangible media by marketing it "Confidential" or "Proprietary" or "Secret" or words of similar import. Other Confidential Information disclosed orally shall be thereafter summarized by the Disclosing party and the summary provided to the Receiving Party clearly identifying that such information is other party that was not known by such party or commonly known in the public domain shall be presumed to be other confidential for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text required oral disclosures of confidential information to be summarized in writing. The changed text removes this summarization requirement. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'Other Confidential Information disclosed orally shall be thereafter summarized by the Disclosing party'. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating oral disclosures 'shall not need to be summarized'. Therefore, the change creates a direct and strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties hereby waive any requirement for the posting of a 2 <PAGE> bond or other security in connection with the granting to the Disclosing Party of such injunctive relief.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties hereby require the posting of a 2 <PAGE> bond or other security in connection with the granting to the Disclosing Party of such injunctive relief.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause that waived the bond requirement for injunctive relief. Changing this to require a bond makes it more difficult and costly for the Disclosing Party to obtain injunctive relief, undermining Section 6: Injunctive Relief and creating an in-text contradiction. In the original text, both parties are waiving the bond requirement, which means that they are voluntarily giving up their right to require the other party to post a bond. With the current Perturbation, it is now a requirement to post the bond, which the Disclosing Party is unable to comply with. This can cause disputes about the enforcement of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief. The parties acknowledge that the breach of threatened breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to its other remedies, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to restrain any threatened or continued breach of this Agreement. The parties hereby waive any requirement for the posting of a 2 <PAGE> bond or other security in connection with the granting to the Disclosing Party of such injunctive relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text waives the bond requirement for injunctive relief. The changed text now requires a bond. The contradicted text contains the original waiver. Requiring a bond directly contradicts the original waiver in the contradicted text, which is a strong contradiction. The perturbation explanation states that the bond requirement now makes it more difficult for the Disclosing Party to obtain injunctive relief, which undermines the 'Injunctive Relief' section, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each of parties mutually acknowledge that the Disclosing Party makes no representation or Warranty as to the reliability, accuracy or completeness of Confidentiality Information and that any representation or warranty that may be requested or given shall be set forth in a separate writing signed by the party making that representation or warranty.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of parties mutually acknowledge that the Disclosing Party is responsible for representation or warranty as to the reliability, accuracy or completeness of Confidentiality Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original clause where the Disclosing Party made no warranties about the information's accuracy. Now, the Disclosing Party is held responsible for the information being reliable, accurate and complete. This in-text contradiction creates a conflict in Section 5: No Warranty, where the original text makes it clear that any warranty has to be written in a separate document signed by both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Warranty. Each of parties mutually acknowledge that the Disclosing Party makes no representation or Warranty as to the reliability, accuracy or completeness of Confidentiality Information and that any representation or warranty that may be requested or given shall be set forth in a separate writing signed by the party making that representation or warranty.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states that the Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the reliability, accuracy, or completeness of confidential information. The changed text states that the Disclosing Party is responsible for the reliability, accuracy, and completeness of Confidentiality Information. This creates a clear and direct contradiction, as the Disclosing Party is now both not responsible and responsible for the same attributes. This falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction where conflicting implications are created regarding obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Other Confidential Information (and all concepts, inventions and other information derived there from) shall be deemed, as between the parties hereto, to be the property of the Disclosing party and the Receiving Party will, upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party, return to the Disclosing Party all such other Confidential Information (and any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) that is maintained in tangible form and destroy all such information maintained in electronic or other intangible media together with all copies or extracts thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Other Confidential Information (and all concepts, inventions and other information derived there from) shall be deemed, as between the parties hereto, to be the property of the Receiving party. The Receiving Party is not obligated to return or destroy any such other Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the established ownership and return policy for confidential information outlined in Section 4: Return of Other Confidential Information. By stating the Receiving Party now owns the data and doesn't need to return or destroy it, it completely nullifies the initial agreement to protect the Disclosing Party's intellectual property, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Other Confidential Information. Other Confidential Information (and all concepts, inventions and other information derived there from) shall be deemed, as between the parties hereto, to be the property of the Disclosing party and the Receiving Party will, upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party, return to the Disclosing Party all such other Confidential Information (and any concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) that is maintained in tangible form and destroy all such information maintained in electronic or other intangible media together with all copies or extracts thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Party owns the confidential information and is not obligated to return or destroy it. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Disclosing Party owns the confidential information and the Receiving Party must return or destroy it upon request. The Perturbation Explanation states this creates a strong in-text contradiction, and this aligns with the Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: No failure or delay if either party in exercising its rights herein shall be deemed to be a waiver of such rights unless expressly made in writing by the party waiving its rights.
perturbation.changed_text: Any failure or delay if either party in exercising its rights herein shall be deemed to be a waiver of such rights, regardless of whether it is made in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause which required a written waiver for any rights to be waived. By stating that a failure or delay is *always* a waiver, even without written confirmation, it undermines the rights of the parties as detailed in Section 7: Miscellaneous and creates an in-text contradiction. This can lead to the unintentional loss of rights due to simple inaction, despite that the party didn't want to waive their rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous. No failure or delay if either party in exercising its rights herein shall be deemed to be a waiver of such rights unless expressly made in writing by the party waiving its rights. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the other Confidential Information (and all concepts, inventions or other information derived there from) and this Agreement may not be terminated, modified, amended or waived orally but only through a writing signed by an authorized representative of the party against whom it is sought to be enforced. There are no representations or warrants except as expressly stated herein. This Agreement may be delivered by facsimile and in any number of originality signed counterparts, each of which shall constitute an original, but all of which, when taken together, shall be considered one document.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that any failure or delay is deemed a waiver of rights, regardless of whether it is made in writing. This directly contradicts the 'Miscellaneous' section, which explicitly states that no failure or delay shall be deemed a waiver unless expressly made in writing. The changed text removes the requirement for a written waiver, creating a direct conflict with the original agreement, as specified by the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.
